channel.title: Errant Thinking - Hexmas
channel.description: Asking insightful questions. Seeking thoughtful answers.

item.title: The Powder Plains
item.author: Errant Thinking
item.description: As part of Prismatic Wasteland's Rankin/Bass-themed 2025 Merry Hexmas, I've contributed the Powder Plains as a snowfield hex. A relatively peaceful snowfield featuring multiple towns including Snowville, a village of cold elementals which neighbors the Snow Henge. January Junction makes an appearance along with Snowdin, inspired from Undertale. Also features Keh Night Automata and a twisted Cult of Janus.

item.title: The Rime Ridge
item.author: Errant Thinking
item.description: As part of Prismatic Wasteland's Rankin/Bass-themed 2025 Merry Hexmas, I present the Rime Ridge, a dense mountain range thick with fog that tends to freeze into hard rime ice upon all surfaces. An icey mountain range inspired by the Misty Mountains of the Hobbit.

item.title: The Sleeping Slopes
item.author: Errant Thinking
item.description:
A jagged mountain range that features a long dormant volcano with a frozen spire at its top. The hollowed out volcano that was once mined for its riches and resources is now abandoned. Perhaps due to the dark secrets within its depths?

This is inspired by the Mines of Moria from "The Lord of the Rings".

item.title: The Vorpal Vale
item.author: Errant Thinking
item.description: A Rankin/Bass themed location for a Merry Hexmas. A mysterious fog covered valley with a winding river between the various hills.

item.title: Hexmas: Four Rankin/Bass Themed Hexes
item.author: Errant Thinking
item.description:
Prismatic Wasteland has kicked off the Blogwagon: Hexmas where he calls for bloggers to create multiple hexes within a shared world inspired by the thematic Christmas cinematic universe of Rankin and Bass, such as the stop motion "Rudolph the Red Nose Reindeer" and the animated "Frosty the Snowman". Rankin/Bass are also responsible for various other animations and stop motion films, including the original Lord of the Rings "Hobbit" and "Return of the King" animated films.

Given this, I provide four hexes to join the existing others, each of which I shall briefly detail here and then later on further refine and develop in their own blog post each following this one.
